text: Designing peptides that accurately interact with bioactive helical proteins, including clinical diagnostics and therapeutics, is a significant challenge. In this study, we develop a set of computational tools leveraging parametric scaffold generation and deep learning, which includes RoseTTAFold (RFjoint) and an extension of the RoseTTAFold diffusion model (RFdiffusion), to design potent peptides that engage their target with picomolar affinity—the highest reported affinity of any computationally designed proteins lacking any information derived from experimental evolution. These peptides are stably folded and highly specific to their cognate protein receptors, comparable to that of antibodies. We validate their application in bioluminescent probes for protein sensors and as capture reagents for sensitive hormone detection in human serum using mass spectrometry. Our research exemplifies the capability of generative deep learning to address intricate molecular recognition problems and confirms that our technology platform is suitable for translating clinical diagnostics for peptide biomarkers into practice.
explanation: The text uses a polished, generic scientific tone with plausible domain terms (RoseTTAFold, RFdiffusion) and lacks experimental detail. The combination of sweeping claims and the absence of verifiable data lean toward AI-generated prose.
